item.title: Alpha - First Contact
item.description: The introductory wargaming challenge requiring a simple /etc/hosts file edit to resolve a custom domain.

item.title: Caesar - Hash Cracking & ROT8000
item.description: A two-part challenge combining SHA256 hash cracking, custom XOR-based decryption, and ROT8000 Unicode rotation to decode a message disguised as East Asian ideograms.

item.title: Confusion - Multi-Layer Steganography with Movie Clues
item.description: A three-part steganography challenge using movie-themed passphrases, steghide extraction, Base64/Base32 decoding, and nested ZIP archives to reconstruct a split flag.

item.title: Emergency Notes - Double XOR Cryptography
item.description: A cryptography challenge focused on the XOR operation and its reversibility, requiring players to test key combinations to decrypt a double-XOR-encrypted backup.

item.title: Football Leaks - Web Vulnerabilities Chain
item.description: A web challenge exploiting client-side authentication bypass, IDOR, and SQL injection to extract three flags from a football data leak portal.

item.title: Handsome Peter - Steganography Challenge
item.description: A multi-technique steganography challenge covering EXIF metadata, file appending, LSB, DCT, colour palette manipulation, and audio steganography.

item.title: LaCasa - FTP, Steganography & Privilege Escalation
item.description: A Money Heist-themed challenge chaining anonymous FTP access, image steganography, SSH lateral movement, and vim-based root privilege escalation.

item.title: Little Juice Challenge - Boot2Root
item.description: A Boot2Root Docker challenge involving port scanning, Git repository exposure, lateral movement via PostgreSQL, and privilege escalation through a misconfigured cronjob.

item.title: Meme Forum - Port Knocking & Password Cracking
item.description: A challenge combining hidden endpoint discovery, port knocking brute force, FTP access, and RAR password cracking with John the Ripper.

item.title: Nicolay Magic Numbers - Network Knocker & Brute Force
item.description: A multi-stage challenge involving port knocking with a Fibonacci sequence, WAF bypass via rate limiting, and a nested zip bomb requiring scripted extraction.

item.title: Otillianni Math - Prime Number Indexing
item.description: A data obfuscation challenge where a flag is hidden inside 10,000 random characters by placing each flag character at prime number indices.

item.title: Reactor - Binary Analysis & XOR Decoding
item.description: A reverse engineering challenge involving string extraction from a binary, hex-to-ASCII decoding, XOR binary-to-decimal conversion, and a two-stage access code challenge.

item.title: Reverse Power - File Carving & Magic Bytes
item.description: A forensics challenge involving corrupted file signatures, hex editing, EXIF password extraction, and password-protected ZIP archives.

item.title: Roda do Hamster - Layered Encoding Decoder
item.description: A challenge solved by scripting a recursive decoder that automatically strips multiple layers of Base16/32/64/85 and Ascii85 encoding until plain text is revealed.

item.title: The Disk Dump - FAT12 Forensics & XOR Decryption
item.description: A forensic imaging challenge involving FAT12 filesystem analysis, recovery of deleted files, a decoy rabbit hole, and XOR-based flag decryption.

item.title: The Moodle Breach - Multi-Vector Forensics
item.description: A miscellaneous challenge combining HTML source analysis, XOR decryption, EXIF metadata extraction, LSB steganography, Base64 decoding, and visual forensics to recover 6 flags.

item.title: The True Owner - LFI, Base64 & Cookie Tampering
item.description: A web challenge chaining OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities: information disclosure via robots.txt, Local File Inclusion, Base64-encoded credentials, and cookie tampering for privilege escalation.

item.title: Homelab #1: Raspberry Pi 4 — Pi-hole, Tailscale & Jellyfin
item.description: How I turned a Raspberry Pi 4 into a home DNS filter, VPN node, and media server.

item.title: Quick Writeup: Network Reconnaissance Analysis with Nmap
item.description: How to identify open ports and vulnerable services on a target host.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Who I Am

I’m Ricardo Ribeiro, a Cybersecurity student from Braga, Portugal. I got into this field because I wanted to understand how systems actually work and how they break. That curiosity led me to a professional course, and eventually to building this space to document the journey.

I’m currently focused on the defensive side. SOC operations, log analysis, and network monitoring while also getting my hands dirty with offensive tools and CTF challenges to understand the attacker’s perspective.
